steps.title: Multiply angle J
steps.content:
Angle $K$ is $4$ times angle $J$, so its radian measure is
$$
4\cdot \frac{7\pi}{11}
$$

steps.title: Convert to degrees
steps.content: To convert radians to degrees, multiply by $\frac{180}{\pi}$: $$ 4\cdot \frac{7\pi}{11}\cdot \frac{180}{\pi} $$ The factors of $\pi$ cancel, leaving $$ \frac{7}{11}(180)(4) $$ The answer is B.
